scenario: Vertex Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 6000 policies per day. After retrying 240 stuck process instances from the Processes view, the operator selects that retry operation from the operations panel and wants to track per-instance progress as the engine works through the selection without leaving Operate or scripting a custom dashboard.
question: Which column appears on the processes instances list once an operation is selected from the operations panel?

options.text: A column named Job Worker Health is added to the list, surfacing each worker's last successful poll timestamp for the running instances
A column named Operation State is added to the processes instances list, indicating the current state of the operation for each instance
A column named Retry Backoff is added to the list, showing the next scheduled retry time for the selected operation on each instance
A column named Incident Owner is added to the list, displaying which operator initially raised the operation for each affected instance

a.text: Incorrect. Selecting an operation from the operations panel does not add a worker health column; that signal lives in cluster health pages, not the processes instances list.

b.text: Correct. After selecting an operation from the operations panel, Operate adds an Operation State column to the processes instances list, indicating the current state of the operation for each instance.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no Retry Backoff column added when an operation is selected; the processes list surfaces the operation state for each instance, not the next retry schedule.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no Incident Owner column added by selecting an operation; the operations panel filter contributes an Operation State column, not an audit field for the originating operator.

explanation: When an operation is selected from the operations panel, the operations panel filter applies and Operate adds an Operation State column to the processes instances list so the operator can see, per instance, the current state of the selected operation across the affected process instances.
